phase_tasks.description: Use the test_driven optimizer to improve the complaint-generator graph analysis phase. Target files: complaint_phases/denoiser.py. Phase goal: Improve knowledge-graph and dependency-graph population so denoising and legal reasoning operate on structured facts instead of raw narrative alone. Recommended actions: Improve denoiser gap selection and answer processing so each turn closes a concrete knowledge-graph gap instead of only restating the narrative.; Make answer processing update entities, relationships, and satisfied requirements deterministically when the complainant supplies the missing field.; Promote exact dates, staff names/titles, hearing-request timing, response dates, and causation sequencing into graph updates before broadening the next question set.. Weak complaint types to generalize for: housing_discrimination, hacc_research_engine. Weak evidence modalities to improve: policy_document, file_evidence. Graph evidence targets: context_or_reason, denial_event, protected_activity. Graph focus areas: claim_elements.

priority_improvements: Improve intake targeting: claim_elements, causation_sequence, conduct
Improve graph element targeting: context_or_reason, denial_event, protected_activity
recommendations: Performance is good. Focus on consistency and edge cases.
Evidence-section coverage is improving. Preserve explicit questioning around: grievance_hearing, appeal_rights.
Knowledge graph gap count is high on average. Improve gap-reduction logic in complaint_phases/denoiser.py and ensure process_answer updates entities/relationships meaningfully.

In 1 sessions, KG gaps did not reduce over iterations. Consider making denoiser.process_answer reduce gaps deterministically (e.g., marking gap items as addressed when answers supply the missing fields).
Knowledge graph relationships are not increasing across iterations. Consider adding relationship updates when answers mention who/what/when/where/why links.
KG gaps are not decreasing on average (or are increasing). Improve gap selection + answer processing so each turn reduces uncertainty.
Dependency satisfaction rate is very low on average. Consider having denoising answers mark requirements as satisfied or add evidence/fact nodes as they are provided.
Add a targeted causation follow-up: ask what specifically links the protected activity to retaliation beyond timing (who said what, when, and any comparator treatment).
Add questions covering intake objectives: causation_sequence
Replace internal jargon with plain-language prompts and break one complex ask into 3 short parts: dates, actors, documents.
Best HACC preset so far is 'core_hacc_policies'. Prefer it when generating evidence-backed adversarial batches.

Graph analysis is repeatedly targeting these claim elements for stronger structure and support propagation: context_or_reason, denial_event, protected_activity. Improve KG/DG updates and denoiser routing for those elements.
Adversarial intake priorities are not fully covered. Add stronger probes or fallback prompts for: causation_sequence (0/1).

Intake questioning is repeatedly targeting these objectives/elements: claim_elements, causation_sequence, conduct, context_or_reason. Improve intake routing, fallback prompts, and legal-element probes for those gaps.
common_weaknesses: The wording included technical/internal phrasing that can confuse complainants and reduce clarity.
It did not directly elicit non-timing causation facts (e.g., statements, comparative treatment, pretext indicators).
common_strengths: The mediator used empathetic language and reduced defensiveness while requesting precision.
The question was focused and high-yield for chronology, producing actionable follow-up leads.
Covered all seeded anchor sections: grievance_hearing, appeal_rights
